meta.name: Gyu Min Lee
meta.title: Postdoctoral Researcher
meta.affiliation: KRIBB (Korea Research Institute of Bioscience and Biotechnology), C1 Refinery Research Center

about.heading: Systems biology, applied to C1 carbon.
about.lede: I work on engineering methanotrophic and methylotrophic bacteria so that methane and methanol can serve as starting carbons for synthetic biology. The wet-lab spine of my work is adaptive laboratory evolution (ALE) coupled with multi-omics sequencing; the read-out is mechanism, not just phenotype. Lately I run an increasing share of that pipeline through AI coding agents, which compresses the slow seam between an experiment finishing and the next one being designed.
paragraphs: My doctoral work at UNIST traced how Methylorubrum extorquens reorganizes its C1 metabolism under sustained methanol stress: 700+ generations of ALE, whole-genome and transcriptome integration, and AlphaFold3 structural analysis to read the convergent mutations as a coherent stress-response network.

At KRIBB I continue along the same axis with new methanotroph hosts, and I build the tooling layer (primer design, RNA-seq pipelines, lab-notebook automation) on top of Claude Code agents so the dry-lab side keeps up with the wet-lab side.

items.institution: Ulsan National Institute of Science and Technology (UNIST)
items.lab: Systems Biology Lab, advisor: Donghyuk Kim, Ph.D.
items.highlight: Dissertation: Systems-level characterization of adaptive responses to methanol stress in Methylorubrum extorquens.
Methanotroph strain engineering, multi-omics pipelines, agentic tooling for the C1 team.
Methylotroph ALE, pan-genome and DEG analysis, bacteriophage and plant-pathogen comparative genomics.
